description: Bill of sale in the hand of Mevorakh b. Natan. Location: Fustat. Dated: Av [14]9[.] Seleucid = 1179–88 CE. This can likely be narrowed to 1179–81 based on the dates of Mevorakh b. Natan. In which the syrup maker (al-sharābī) Yosef b. ʿUlla b. Yosef sells to the perfumer (al-ʿaṭṭār) Abū l-Makārim Nadiv b. Elʿazar 1/8 of a house in the Surriyya lane, a blind alley in the Sūq al-Kabīr, which was known as the hall in which Abū l-Maḥāsin Zayn al-Ghuzūlī al-Levi lived. Price: 7.5 dinars. Other proprietors were Christians (1/2 of the house), and Abū l-Munajjā al-Levi al-Zayyāt known as Ibn Jallāb (1/6 of the house). With a lot of legal verbiage in all three languages. Witnesses: (Abū Sahl) Levi b. Avraham ha-Levi; Avraham b. Yaḥyā ha-Levi; Shelomo b. Natan; Shemuel b. Yosef. Witnesses to the validation: [...] b. Saʿadya; Elʿazar b. [...]; and Mevorakh b. Natan. (Information in part from Goitein's index card and from Mediterranean Society, IV, 281.)
description_he: מסמך משפטי בAramaic, Hebrew, Judaeo-Arabic מהמאה ה-12. מוחזק בספריית קיימברידג'.
